message:
feat(ffs-mvcc): fix chain trimming, improve commit error handling, and harden persistence

Return the Transaction alongside CommitError from commit_fcw_internal and
commit_ssi_internal so callers can recover or inspect the failed transaction
state rather than losing it on conflict. This is necessary for the
reordered journal write path in ffs-core where MVCC commit must precede
JBD2 writes.

Fix chain trimming: call make_chain_head_full() BEFORE draining the
version chain rather than after. The old order called ensure_chain_head_full
on an already-trimmed chain, which could not resolve Identical references
because their referents had already been removed. Rename to
make_chain_head_full(versions, keep_from) with an explicit index parameter
for clarity. Apply the same fix to sharded MVCC store.

Snapshot registry: only reset the oldest-active-snapshot tracking when the
released snapshot was actually the oldest, preventing unnecessary
recomputation when middle or newest snapshots are released.

Expose maybe_dedup() as pub(crate) so the persistence layer can apply
dedup during WAL replay and checkpoint loading, maintaining the same
memory-efficient Identical representation used by live commits.

Persistence: add file-length validation when loading checkpoint data_len
fields to detect truncated or corrupt checkpoint files early. Cap initial
Vec allocation for versions and WAL writes to 1024 to prevent
allocation-bomb attacks from malformed data. Apply dedup during both
checkpoint loading and WAL replay when the compression policy enables it,
reducing memory footprint of restored state.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
